q: The study of interference and diffraction deals with _______.
_______ optics is also known as physical optics.
In 1678, Christiaan Huygens introduced the _______ theory of light.
Every point on a wavefront may be a source of the _______ wavelets.
A _______ is a surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all the points in the wave that have the same phase.
On a particular wavefront, at a given moment, all the particles of the medium are undergoing the same _______.
The wavefront is always _______ to the direction of the propagation.
The spherical wavefront becomes a _______ wavefront when far from the source.
The _______ angle is the position of a point on a reference circle of a waveform at an instant.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is called the _______ angle.
The difference in states of the periodic waves at the same instant is represented by the _______ difference.
The _______ difference indicates the distance between two points measured along the direction of propagation of the wave through the medium.
Wave _______ occurs when two waves meet while travelling along the same medium.
If light waves interfere with one another, the maximum and the minimum _______ of light are obtained alternately.
To observe the interference of light waves, the two sources of light must be _______ with respect to each other.
In 1801, Thomas Young obtained convincing evidence for the _______ nature of light.
In a double-slit experiment, a series of alternative bright and dark lines are seen on the _______.
A series of alternative bright and dark lines in the double-slit experiment are the result of the _______ phenomenon.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, a _______ wavelet emerges from the slit.
The _______ wavelet is emitted from a point on the plane wave according to Huygens' principle.
When a circular wave passes through two slits, each slit acts as a _______ source of circular waves.
In a double-slit experiment, the _______ pattern will be displayed on the screen.
In a double-slit experiment, the interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called _______.
In a double-slit experiment, _______ interference occurs at the centre of the screen.
The bright fringe at the centre of the screen is called the _______ bright line.
If the path difference equals an integral number of wavelengths, the waves will interfere _______.
If the waves interfere constructively, a _______ fringe appears on the screen.
If the path difference is an odd integral multiple of half-wavelength, the waves will interfere _______.
If the waves interfere destructively, a _______ fringe appears on the screen.
_______ interference results in bright fringes.
_______ interference results in dark fringes.
The position of particular bright and dark fringes on the screen is given by the distance from the _______ bright fringe.
The zeroth order bright corresponds to the _______ bright line at $\theta=0$.
The first-order bright fringes form on either side of the _______ line.
For a _______ fringe to be formed at a point, the path difference is an odd integral multiple of half wavelength.
In a double-slits pattern, the value of m for the central bright fringe is _______.
In a double-slits pattern, the value of m for the first bright fringe is _______.
In a double-slits pattern, the value of m for the second bright fringe is _______.
In a double-slits pattern, the value of m for the first dark fringe is _______.
In a double-slits pattern, the value of m for the second dark fringe is _______.
The distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the _______ width.
The intensity of the dark fringe is _______.
In diffraction, the amount of bending depends on the _______ of light relative to the size of the aperture.
In diffraction, if the aperture is much _______ than the wavelength of light, the bending will be almost unnoticeable.
Light waves _______ around the objects and create bright and dark fringes.
In the Fresnel diffraction, there is a/an _______ distance between the source and the screen from the obstacle.
In the Fraunhofer diffraction, there is a/an _______ distance between the source and the screen from the obstacle.
In a single slit diffraction, the diffraction pattern consists of a _______ central portion.
In a single slit diffraction, on either side of the central maximum, there are alternating bright and dark _______.
In a single slit diffraction, the bright central portion is called the central _______.
In a single slit diffraction, the bright and dark fringes are created by the _______ diffracted light waves.
In a single slit diffraction, each successive bright fringe becomes _______ intense proceeding outward.
In a single slit diffraction, the first _______ are seen on either side of the central bright maximum.
In a single slit diffraction, the position of a certain minimum is the distance from the _______ maximum.
The width of the central maximum is _______ times the position of the first minimum.
Most modern-day applications of slit interference use _______.
The diffraction grating is an important tool in _______ analysis.
A diffraction grating consists of a large number of _______ slits of the same width separated by equal opaque spaces.
A diffraction grating can disperse light into its _______ components.
The diffraction grating is used to resolve light into _______.
Diffraction grating is widely used as a _______ element in spectrographic instruments.
A compact disc is an example of _______ gratings.
A reflection grating is a _______ surface having many fine grooves.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a diffraction grating, producing a separation of the colours of _______ light.
